nameMeanings.name: Browne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably also an Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Braune   or   Braun  .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

variants.name: Brown
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: generally a nickname referring to the color of the hair or complexion, Middle English br(o)un, from Old English brūn or Old French brun. This word is occasionally found in Old French, Middle English and Old Norse as a personal name or byname (Middle English personal name Brun, Broun, ancient Germanic Bruno, Old English Brūn, or possibly Old Norse Brúnn or Brúni). Brun- was also an ancient Germanic name-forming element. Some instances of Old English Brūn as a personal name may therefore be short forms of compound names such as Brūngar, Brūnwine, etc. As a Scottish and Irish name, it sometimes represents a translation of Gaelic Donn (see below). Brown (including in the senses below) is the fourth most frequent surname in the US. It is also very common among African Americans and Native Americans (see also 5 below).
Irish and Scottish: adopted for Ó Duinn (see Dunn ) or for any of the many Irish and Scottish Gaelic names containing the element donn ‘brown-haired’ (also meaning ‘chieftain’), for example Donahue .
Irish: phonetic Anglicization of Mac an Bhreitheamhnaigh; see Breheny .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When William Browne was born about 1442, in Betchworth, Surrey, England, his father, Thomas Browne, was 21 and his mother, Lady Eleanor Arundel, was 29. He had at least 2 sons with Mary Malet. He died before 1483, and was buried in Calais, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Browne est né environ 1442, à Betchworth, Surrey, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Browne, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Lady Eleanor Arundel, avait 29 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Mary Malet. Il est décédé avant 1483, et a été enterré à Calais, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Browne um 1442 in Betchworth, Surrey, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Browne 21 und seine Mutter Lady Eleanor Arundel 29. Er hatte mit Mary Malet mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb vor 1483 und wurde in Calais, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Browne è nato il circa 1442 nel Betchworth, Surrey, England, il suo padre, Thomas Browne, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Lady Eleanor Arundel, aveva 29 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Mary Malet. Morì prima del 1483, e fu sepolto a Calais, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia.

ko.lifeSummary: William Browne(이)가 대략 1442년 영국 수리 베트ch워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Browne(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Eleanor Arundel(은)는 29세였다. 그는 Mary Malet(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1483년 이전 사망했으며 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 파스-데-캘레이 캘레이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Browne родился около 1442 г. в местности бечворт, сурри, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Browne было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Eleanor Arundel, 29. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Mary Malet. Он умер перед 1483 г. и был похоронен в местности калеи, пас-де-калеи, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция.
